nodes.label: AI as admin task handler

nodes.label: AI as iteration tool for variations
nodes.label_negative: AI as unpredictable and hard to direct

nodes.label: AI as cost-saving substitute for human labor
nodes.label_negative: AI as threat to established illustrators' styles
nodes.grounding_span: its only truly useful if there's no budget for hiring a human illustrator

nodes.label: AI as improving technical tool (e.g., cutouts, generative fill)
nodes.grounding_span: I look forward to Photoshop's integrated AI tools improving

nodes.label: Creative control and intentionality
nodes.grounding_span: it's difficult to 'art direct' the various models

nodes.label: Efficiency and stress reduction in admin
nodes.grounding_span: taking the stress out of various admin-related aspects of creative work

nodes.label: Economic pragmatism
nodes.grounding_span: no budget for hiring a human illustrator

nodes.label: Artistic integrity and originality
nodes.grounding_span: established illustrators who have had their distinctive style 'ripped' by AI models

nodes.label: Positive toward AI for admin tasks
nodes.grounding_span: using LLMs has been tremendously helpful in taking the stress out of various admin-related aspects

nodes.label: Negative toward AI for genuine creative work
nodes.grounding_span: for genuinely creative work, AI feels like an impediment of sorts

nodes.label: Ambivalent toward AI as iteration tool
nodes.grounding_span: using AI feels like a bit of a crapshoot

nodes.label: Positive anticipation for AI tool improvement
nodes.grounding_span: I look forward to Photoshop's integrated AI tools improving

nodes.label: Domain-specific pragmatism — evaluates AI utility per creative domain
nodes.grounding_span: I don't see photography as immediately being as negatively affected by AI as much as illustration

nodes.label: Iterative hybrid workflow — alternates between hand-drawn and AI
nodes.grounding_span: hand drawn image that was modified in Firefly, iterated on, then redrawn again by hand
